sanskrit:
या निशा सर्वभूतानां तस्यां जागर्ति संयमी।
यस्यां जाग्रति भूतानि सा निशा पश्यतो मुनेः॥
transliteration: yā niśā sarva-bhūtānāṃ tasyāṃ jāgarti saṃyamī | yasyāṃ jāgrati bhūtāni sā niśā paśyato muneḥ ||
literal_meaning: What is night for all beings, in that the self-controlled one is awake; in which all beings are awake, that is night for the seeing sage.
contextual_meaning: The verse maps two mutually-inverted realities: the collective ‘day’ of material pursuits is the sage’s ‘night’ of unconsciousness, while the collective ‘night’ of withdrawal is the sage’s ‘day’ of awakened Self-awareness. It reframes social norms as hypnotic trance and solitude as lucidity.
psychological_state: Depersonalization from consensus reality; shift from extrinsic to intrinsic locus of evaluation—akin to waking up inside a dream and realizing everyone else is still dreaming.
trigger_scenario: You leave a high-status career party at 2 a.m., sober, while colleagues keep drinking. Walking home, the city’s neon buzz suddenly feels like sleep-talk and your private journaling becomes the only awake space on the planet.
anchor_text: When others chase the world, wake within; when they wake, stay inwardly still.
